1.title: Import and profile operational data in Power BI
1.concepts: Power Query import, data types, column profiling, source documentation
1.evidence_skill: Power BI data preparation and profiling
1.label: Complete Applied Lab 01: Import and profile data in Power BI
aliases: Learn Power BI data loading and relationships
1.objective: Load operational exports, inspect their grain and quality, and document what must be corrected before modeling.
steps: Import the supplied projects, shots, and time-entry files.
Assign data types and use Power Query profiling views.
Document missing values, duplicates, suspicious ranges, and source grain.
Rename queries and fields for stakeholder readability.
deliverables: Power BI file or screenshots of the imported queries.
Profiling table with issue, impact, and proposed fix.
validation: Every table has a stated grain and candidate key.
Dates and numeric fields use correct data types.
At least three quality observations are supported by evidence.

2.title: Clean, merge, append, pivot, and unpivot with Power Query
2.concepts: Power Query cleaning, merge, append, pivot, unpivot
2.label: Complete Applied Lab 02: Transform data with Power Query
2.objective: Create a repeatable transformation flow that converts raw exports into analysis-ready tables.
steps: Trim and standardize text and replace invalid blanks with nulls.
Merge shots to projects and verify join cardinality.
Name and document every transformation step.
deliverables: Explanation of why the long target table is easier to analyze.
validation: The merge does not duplicate shot grain.
The appended total equals both inputs combined.
The unpivoted data reconciles to the wide source.

3.title: Build a star schema and date table
3.concepts: fact and dimension tables, relationships, date table, filter direction
3.label: Complete Applied Lab 03: Build a Power BI star schema
3.objective: Model the cleaned data at a clear grain using facts and dimensions.
steps: Define the grain of shot and time-entry facts.
Create project, artist, department, status, and date dimensions.
Mark the date table and hide technical fields.
validation: A project filter affects both facts correctly.

4.title: Create reusable DAX measures
4.concepts: CALCULATE, DIVIDE, conditional logic, variance, time comparison
4.label: Complete Applied Lab 04: Build and validate DAX measures
4.objective: Create a reusable measure layer and independently validate its results.
steps: Create total hours, shot count, completion rate, and average-hours measures.
Create budget and schedule variance measures.
deliverables: Validation table comparing measures to source totals.

5.title: Build an executive report with guided interactions
5.concepts: visual hierarchy, slicers, drill-through, tooltips, accessibility
5.evidence_skill: Power BI report design and data storytelling
5.label: Complete Applied Lab 05: Build an executive Power BI report
5.objective: Turn the model into a concise report that supports a named management decision.
steps: Define the audience, decision, and three questions.
Build an overview page with clear visual hierarchy.
Add useful slicers, drill-through, and a tooltip page.
Check titles, units, contrast, alt text, and layout.
Remove visuals that do not support a question.
validation: Every visual answers a named question.
The report fits common laptop resolution.

6.title: Document publishing, refresh, and row-level security
6.concepts: Power BI Service, refresh planning, ownership, row-level security
6.evidence_skill: Power BI deployment and governance planning
6.label: Complete Applied Lab 06: Plan Power BI publishing and security
6.objective: Document how a real report would be published, refreshed, owned, and secured.
steps: Define refresh frequency and failure handling.
Draft an RLS rule and test matrix.
Assign report owner, backup owner, and data steward.

7.title: Build an end-to-end Excel analyst workbook
7.concepts: tables, validation, XLOOKUP, SUMIFS, PivotTables, Power Query, controls
7.evidence_skill: Excel analysis, controls, and management reporting
7.label: Complete Applied Lab 07: Build an Excel analyst workbook
7.objective: Build a controlled workbook that cleans data, calculates reliable metrics, and presents a management summary.
steps: Load CSV files into named tables.
Add data validation and controlled inputs.
Use Power Query to clean and combine sources.
Use XLOOKUP, SUMIFS, and explicit error handling.
Build PivotTables, PivotCharts, slicers, and a one-page summary.
Create a controls and assumptions sheet.
validation: A new month can be added through documented refresh steps.

8.title: Load and profile data with pandas
8.concepts: read_csv, dtypes, shape, missing values, duplicates, statistics
8.label: Complete Applied Lab 08: Load and profile data with pandas
aliases: Complete a Python and pandas fundamentals lesson
8.objective: Use a reproducible script to inspect data before cleaning.
steps: Print shape, columns, dtypes, and samples.
Measure nulls, duplicates, and unexpected categories.
validation: The script reruns without manual edits.

9.title: Clean and merge operational tables with pandas
9.concepts: string cleaning, dates, missing values, merge validation, duplicate control
9.label: Complete Applied Lab 09: Clean and merge data with pandas
aliases: Load and inspect a dataset with pandas
9.objective: Create a clean analysis table while protecting its intended grain.
steps: Merge orders, customers, products, and returns with validation.
validation: One row per intended order line.
Excluded rows are counted and explained.

10.title: Aggregate and export analysis results with pandas
10.concepts: groupby, agg, pivot_table, calculated fields, export
10.label: Complete Applied Lab 10: Aggregate and export pandas results
10.objective: Produce reusable summary tables for revenue, returns, service levels, and segments.
steps: Create grouped revenue and order summaries.
Calculate return rate from explicit numerator and denominator.
Add a ranked or threshold field.
validation: Rates use summed numerators and denominators.

11.title: Reproduce a SQL analysis in pandas
11.concepts: SQL-to-pandas translation, joins, groupby, parity testing
11.label: Complete Applied Lab 11: Reproduce SQL results in pandas
11.objective: Translate a completed SQL analysis into pandas and prove both results match.
steps: Implement the same logic in pandas.
Normalize sort order, types, and rounding.
Compare keys, rows, and numeric differences programmatically.
validation: More than row count is compared.

12.title: Write an executive summary and stakeholder memo
12.concepts: problem, finding, impact, recommendation, limitation, audience adaptation
12.label: Complete Applied Lab 12: Write an executive summary and stakeholder memo
12.objective: Translate analysis into a decision-oriented message for a nontechnical manager.
steps: Write five sentences: problem, finding, impact, recommendation, limitation.
Expand them into a one-page memo.
Use one visual only if it improves the decision.

13.title: Prepare a dashboard walkthrough and stakeholder Q&A
13.concepts: five-minute walkthrough, narrative order, likely questions, evidence-based answers
13.label: Complete Applied Lab 13: Prepare a dashboard walkthrough and Q&A
aliases: Review the project with a stakeholder mindset
13.objective: Explain a dashboard in five minutes and prepare for likely manager questions.
steps: Choose the three visuals to show first.
validation: Answers distinguish fact, inference, and recommendation.

14.title: Create a decision log and limitations register
14.concepts: metric definitions, exclusions, thresholds, assumptions, limitations
14.label: Complete Applied Lab 14: Document analytical decisions and limitations
14.objective: Make analytical choices reviewable and transparent.
steps: Assign follow-up actions to unresolved assumptions.
validation: Every KPI has a definition and rationale.
Open assumptions have owners or actions.

15.title: Build and apply a SQL validation checklist
15.concepts: row counts, uniqueness, nulls, referential integrity, duplicates, reconciliation, boundaries
15.label: Complete Applied Lab 15: Apply the SQL validation checklist
15.objective: Create reusable checks that must pass before results are trusted.
steps: Check source and output row counts.
Test date boundaries and explain the result.

16.title: Diagnose duplicated revenue from a broken join
16.concepts: join cardinality, grain, duplicate amplification, reconciliation
16.label: Complete Applied Lab 16: Fix a join that duplicates revenue
16.objective: Find why a plausible query overstates revenue and correct it without hiding duplicates.
validation: DISTINCT is not used as an unexplained patch.
The exact cardinality error is named.

17.title: Fix denominator, date-filter, and grain errors
17.concepts: KPI denominator, inclusive dates, weighted rates, analysis grain
17.label: Complete Applied Lab 17: Repair misleading KPI calculations
17.objective: Repair a wrong denominator, incomplete date filter, and average-of-averages error.
steps: Replace average-of-averages with a weighted result.
Compare business interpretation before and after.

18.title: Repair a misleading analytical story
18.concepts: chart scale, cherry-picking, average of averages, annotation, uncertainty
18.evidence_skill: Detecting misleading visual and narrative choices
18.label: Complete Applied Lab 18: Repair a misleading chart and narrative
18.objective: Identify how a chart and narrative exaggerate a change and rebuild them with context.
steps: List misleading visual and statistical choices.
Choose an appropriate scale and period.
Add a meaningful limitation or annotation.

19.title: Answer a missed-deadlines request in 30 minutes
19.concepts: rapid scoping, SQL analysis, validation, concise recommendation
19.label: Complete Applied Lab 19: Timed analysis of missed deadlines
19.objective: Answer a manager who needs the top causes of missed deadlines within 30 minutes.
steps: Find the top three causes and contribution.
Write a three-sentence conclusion and action.

20.title: Reconcile a finance total discrepancy
20.concepts: reconciliation, scope differences, exclusions, grain, audit trail
20.label: Complete Applied Lab 20: Reconcile conflicting finance totals
20.objective: Explain why a dashboard differs from a finance report and produce an auditable bridge.
steps: Compare scope, dates, grain, filters, and currency.
validation: Confirmed and open causes are separated.

21.title: Respond to a request for a misleading metric
21.concepts: metric risk, stakeholder clarification, safer alternative, ethical communication
21.evidence_skill: Stakeholder challenge and responsible metric design
21.label: Complete Applied Lab 21: Propose a safer alternative metric
21.objective: Handle a metric request that could encourage the wrong behavior or misrepresent performance.
steps: Restate the requested metric and decision.
Propose a safer metric or balanced set.
validation: New metric reduces the identified risk.
